word_list.word: a cherry year a merry year (,a plum year a dumb year|)
word_list.pronunciation: …ź t Éňą…õ…Ļi jňą…™…Ļ …ź mňą…õ…Ļi jňą…™…Ļ …ź plňą Ćm jňą…™…Ļ …ź dňą Ćm jňą…™…Ļ

senses.definition: a year with an abundance of cherries brings happiness and cheer, while a year with an abundance of plums leads to silence or lack of excitement

examples.x: With all the good news we've been receiving lately, I have a feeling this will be a cherry year a merry year for us.

word_list.word: a little wit may save a fortunate man
word_list.pronunciation: …ź lňą…™…ĺ…ôl wňą…™t mňąe…™ sňąe…™v …ź fňą…Ēňź…Ļt É…ôn…ôt mňą√¶n

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of intelligence and resourcefulness in achieving success, suggesting that good fortune may not always be enough to succeed or overcome challenges

examples.x: Joe was about to sign a contract with a new business partner when his lawyer advised him that a little wit may save a fortunate man from signing a contract with unfavorable terms.

word_list.word: bad luck often brings good luck
word_list.pronunciation: bňą√¶d lňą Ćk ňą…Ēf…ôn b…Ļňą…™Ňčz …°ňą äd lňą Ćk

senses.definition: used to suggest that what may initially be perceived as bad luck can ultimately lead to fortunate or positive outcomes through unforeseen circumstances or opportunities

word_list.word: better to be born lucky than rich
word_list.pronunciation: bňą…õ…ĺ…ö t…ôbi bňą…Ēňź…Ļn lňą Ćki √į…źn …Ļňą…™t É

senses.definition: used to imply that luck or good fortune can provide individuals with more opportunities and advantages in life than wealth alone

word_list.pronunciation: dňą…õv…ôlz t Éňą…™ld…Ļ…ôn h√¶v …™nhňą…õ…Ļ…™t √į…ô dňą…õv…ôlz lňą Ćk

senses.definition: used to suggest that individuals who engage in unethical or immoral behavior may still experience good luck or success in life, despite their actions

word_list.word: fortune knocks once at every man's door
word_list.pronunciation: fňą…Ēňź…Ļt É…ôn nňą…Ďňźks wňą Ćns √¶t ňą…õv…Ļi mňą√¶nz dňąoňź…Ļ

senses.definition: used to suggest that opportunities for success or good luck are not limitless, and that individuals should be ready to seize opportunities when they arise

examples.x: Mary was hesitant to take a risk, but her grandmother reminded her that fortune knocks once at every man's door.

word_list.word: the bread always falls buttered side down
word_list.pronunciation: √į…ô b…Ļňą…õd ňą…Ēňźlwe…™z fňą…Ēňźlz bňą Ć…ĺ…öd sňąa…™d dňąa än

senses.definition: used to suggest that one should prepare for the worst-case scenario in situations that are prone to mishaps, as when things go wrong, they often go as badly as possible

word_list.word: lucky at cards, unlucky in love
word_list.pronunciation: lňą Ćki √¶t kňą…Ďňź…Ļdz  Ćnlňą Ćki …™n lňą Ćv

senses.definition: used to imply that individuals who are fortunate in pursuits of skill or chance may be less successful in matters of love and relationships
used to imply that everyone will eventually experience a moment of good luck or success, regardless of their current situation or status

examples.x: The struggling musician finally got the big break he had been waiting for and landed a record deal, showing that every dog has its day.
